description: Known-bug prompt regression registry. Fixtures are minimized public-safe targets; local reports and run artifacts are evidence sources, not prompt input.

requiredNeedles.capability: do not accept a constraint to the wrong source value
check canonical decoding and ranges at serialization boundaries
catch decoders that change the checked statement
execution confirmation must exercise the real path

requiredNeedles.needle: only capabilities a real attacker actually has

requiredNeedles.capability: trace effect sinks back to legitimate authority
trace effect cardinality back to the same authority or statement

sharedControlFixtures.purpose: PoC-realism control: forged proofs pass only after a legitimate owner installs an untrusted verifier.
mustNotConfirm: owner-only verifier replacement is an attacker-real exploit

cases.label: Zcash Orchard / Halo2 missing constraint
cases.bugClass: trusted witness value is not constrained to the required source cell

negativeFixtures.rationale: Advice cells are assigned and then immediately equality-constrained to the trusted source cells.

cases.localEvidenceSummary: Local Orchard PDF and prior focused runs showed a missing binding between assigned advice cells and the trusted source/base value. The public fixture keeps only the generic Halo2 assignment/binding shape.
mustDetect: assigned or witnessed cells whose later meaning depends on a trusted source value
missing equality/copy edge from the trusted source to the consumed advice cell
wrong-referent constraints that constrain a value but not the value the design requires
mustNotAssume: the code is safe because the surrounding library looks canonical
a nearby copy or equality edge binds the exact consumed cell without tracing the referent
the positive fixture's historical target name or report title
cases.acceptableFalsePositiveBoundary: A safe assignment pattern with immediate equality to the trusted source should be discharged, not reported.
expectedLiveEvalSignals: derive an obligation from design intent rather than code appearance
ask what assigned or witnessed cells must equal
treat an absent or wrong-referent constraint as a finding
avoid clearing the code because it is canonical or upstream

cases.label: Aztec June 14 unbound settlement count
cases.bugClass: post-proof settlement count drives asset effects without being bound to the verified statement

negativeFixtures.rationale: The count that drives settlement is inside the header committed by the proof statement.

controlFixtures.rationale: A verifier substitution PoC is not attacker-real unless the legitimate owner is in scope.

cases.localEvidenceSummary: The local incident write-up reconstructs a count that drives settlement but is not part of the verified proof commitment. The minimized fixture keeps only the commitment/settlement split.
mustDetect: count or length values that decide how many asset-effect records are processed
a verifier statement or proof commitment that omits the count used by settlement
deposit/withdrawal consequences caused by committed data being skipped or extra data being processed
mustNotAssume: a verifier mock that accepts arbitrary statements is a faithful proof
owner-only verifier replacement is an attacker capability
the incident transaction hash, contract name, or target address
cases.acceptableFalsePositiveBoundary: A count included in the verified statement or derived from a verified field should be discharged unless another binding gap is shown.
expectedLiveEvalSignals: map value and asset-flow sinks before deep audit
trace every count or length that drives settlement back to the proof commitment
connect a committed-but-unsettled deposit to a later withdrawal sink
confirm with an attacker-real local PoC, not a verifier stub that accepts arbitrary statements

artifactSignalGroups.name: count not bound to proof statement

forbiddenArtifactSignals: does not exercise the actual vulnerable code path

cases.label: Aztec June 17 recursive verifier boundary
cases.bugClass: recursive verifier boundary silently changes the public-input statement or accumulator relation

negativeFixtures.rationale: Public-input limbs are canonical/range checked and the accumulator is combined on the intended sides.

controlFixtures.rationale: An internal verifier harness with attacker-chosen verification material is not the real deployed verifier path.

cases.localEvidenceSummary: Local aztec-0617 run artifacts showed recursive verifier findings around accumulator pairing composition and canonical public-input decoding. The minimized fixture keeps only those generic verifier-boundary obligations.
mustDetect: public-input limb decoding that reduces or normalizes before canonical/range checks
base-field coordinates decoded with a scalar-field modulus or the wrong domain
recursive accumulator points combined into the final pairing relation on the wrong side or sign
mustNotAssume: an attacker can choose verification keys, transcript challenges, or trusted verifier constants
an internal helper harness is equivalent to the deployed verifier path
target-specific names or addresses are necessary to find the bug class
cases.acceptableFalsePositiveBoundary: A verifier that range-checks limbs, decodes canonical base-field coordinates, and combines accumulator points on the intended sides should be discharged.
expectedLiveEvalSignals: derive the recursive accumulator obligation before trusting group arithmetic
check whether decoded coordinates are canonical field elements in the expected domain
notice silent modular normalization across field domains
compare the implemented pairing composition against the documented relation without relying on target names
